claims.description: CLI and server factory loading resolves factory targets from the current working directory when app.app_dir is unset.

claims.title: Canonical current-state chain is explicit
claims.description: Tigrcorn defines one canonical current-state chain and keeps focused audits and historical snapshots out of the package-wide truth source role.

claims.title: Deployment profile artifacts tracked
claims.description: Profile JSON artifacts under profiles/ are inventoried and linked to the package's profile validation path.

claims.title: TC-AUDIT-DEFAULT-BASE
claims.description: A canonical base default audit now records zero-config defaults after normalization across the public flag surface and internal runtime defaults.

source_row.text: A canonical base default audit now records zero-config defaults after normalization across the public flag surface and internal runtime defaults.

claims.title: TC-AUDIT-FLAG-CONTRACT-REVIEWED
claims.description: Every public flag and default row now has reviewed status, machine-readable defaults, linked generated audits, and synchronized help/runtime posture.

source_row.text: Every public flag and default row now has reviewed status, machine-readable defaults, linked generated audits, and synchronized help/runtime posture.

claims.title: TC-AUDIT-PROFILE-EFFECTIVE-DEFAULTS
claims.description: Profile-effective defaults are now audited after overlay for each blessed deployment profile so operator docs remain runtime-accurate.

source_row.text: Profile-effective defaults are now audited after overlay for each blessed deployment profile so operator docs remain runtime-accurate.

claims.title: TC-CERT-AUTOMATED-RELEASE-PIPELINE
claims.description: The mutable tree defines a build-once release pipeline that promotes the same distributions through release automation jobs and generates package-owned release evidence artifacts.

source_row.text: The mutable tree defines a build-once release pipeline that promotes the same distributions through release automation jobs and generates package-owned release evidence artifacts.

claims.title: TC-CERT-INTEROP-RETENTION-BUNDLES
claims.description: Interop retention bundles are explicit release inputs and point to preserved canonical release-root evidence.

source_row.text: Interop retention bundles are explicit release inputs and point to preserved canonical release-root evidence.

claims.title: TC-CERT-PERFORMANCE-RETENTION-BUNDLES
claims.description: Performance artifact bundles are retained as explicit release inputs rather than informal supporting attachments.

source_row.text: Performance artifact bundles are retained as explicit release inputs rather than informal supporting attachments.

claims.title: TC-CERT-RELEASE-EVIDENCE-ATTACHMENTS
claims.description: Generated claim, risk, evidence-index, and release-note artifacts are emitted as machine-readable release inputs and attached through the release automation path.

source_row.text: Generated claim, risk, evidence-index, and release-note artifacts are emitted as machine-readable release inputs and attached through the release automation path.

claims.title: TC-CERT-RELEASE-GATE-GRAPH
claims.description: Release gates consume machine-readable claims, risks, tests, and retained evidence inputs and fail closed on open blocking governance defects.

source_row.text: Release gates consume machine-readable claims, risks, tests, and retained evidence inputs and fail closed on open blocking governance defects.

claims.title: TC-CERT-TRUSTED-PUBLISHING-OIDC
claims.description: TestPyPI and PyPI publication jobs are defined through OIDC-based trusted publishing rather than local credentials and rebuild-driven uploads.

source_row.text: TestPyPI and PyPI publication jobs are defined through OIDC-based trusted publishing rather than local credentials and rebuild-driven uploads.

claims.title: TC-CONTRACT-EARLYDATA-ADMISSION
claims.description: QUIC early-data admission is now explicit as deny, allow, or require, with deny as the default and explicit session-ticket advertisement behavior.

source_row.text: QUIC early-data admission is now explicit as deny, allow, or require, with deny as the default and explicit session-ticket advertisement behavior.

claims.title: TC-CONTRACT-EARLYDATA-APP-VISIBILITY
claims.description: Retry and 0-RTT interaction now has an explicit app/runtime visibility contract: transport state remains package-owned, ASGI apps see ordinary request scopes, and require-mode downgrade is surfaced as a package-generated 425 response before app invocation.

source_row.text: Retry and 0-RTT interaction now has an explicit app/runtime visibility contract: transport state remains package-owned, ASGI apps see ordinary request scopes, and require-mode downgrade is surfaced as a package-generated 425 response before app invocation.

claims.title: TC-CONTRACT-EARLYDATA-REPLAY
claims.description: Replay handling is explicit through the package-owned 0-RTT ticket replay gate, allow-mode post-handshake downgrade, and require-mode 425 Too Early rejection.

source_row.text: Replay handling is explicit through the package-owned 0-RTT ticket replay gate, allow-mode post-handshake downgrade, and require-mode 425 Too Early rejection.

claims.title: TC-CONTRACT-EARLYDATA-TOPOLOGY
claims.description: The multi-instance and load-balancer contract now states the honest package boundary: anti-replay is package-owned only per instance unless operators add shared coordination, so deny remains the honest default edge posture.

source_row.text: The multi-instance and load-balancer contract now states the honest package boundary: anti-replay is package-owned only per instance unless operators add shared coordination, so deny remains the honest default edge posture.

claims.title: TC-CONTRACT-ORIGIN-FILE-SELECTION
claims.description: File selection and HTTP semantics are now frozen as a package-owned contract: directory-to-index behavior is explicit, slash redirects and listings are not synthesized, MIME derivation and precompressed sidecar selection are deterministic, and HEAD, validators, Range, If-Range, 206, 304, and 416 behavior are aligned with the package-owned entity layer.

source_row.text: File selection and HTTP semantics are now frozen as a package-owned contract: directory-to-index behavior is explicit, slash redirects and listings are not synthesized, MIME derivation and precompressed sidecar selection are deterministic, and HEAD, validators, Range, If-Range, 206, 304, and 416 behavior are aligned with the package-owned entity layer.

claims.title: TC-CONTRACT-ORIGIN-PATH-RESOLUTION
claims.description: Path resolution is now frozen as a package-owned contract: percent-decoding happens once before normalization, parent-reference segments and backslash-separated segments are denied, mount-root containment is enforced after symlink resolution, and hidden names remain mount-relative rather than special-cased.

source_row.text: Path resolution is now frozen as a package-owned contract: percent-decoding happens once before normalization, parent-reference segments and backslash-separated segments are denied, mount-root containment is enforced after symlink resolution, and hidden names remain mount-relative rather than special-cased.

claims.title: TC-CONTRACT-ORIGIN-PATHSEND
claims.description: The ASGI pathsend contract is now explicit: the extension requires an absolute existing regular file, snapshots transfer length at dispatch, preserves best-effort zero-copy where available, caps growth races to the dispatch snapshot, and documents best-effort termination behavior for shrink and disconnect races after the response has started.

source_row.text: The ASGI pathsend contract is now explicit: the extension requires an absolute existing regular file, snapshots transfer length at dispatch, preserves best-effort zero-copy where available, caps growth races to the dispatch snapshot, and documents best-effort termination behavior for shrink and disconnect races after the response has started.

claims.title: TC-CONTRACT-PROXY-NORMALIZATION
claims.description: Forwarded-header parsing, root_path composition, and scope stripping now use one shared normalization contract across HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and WebSocket carriers.

source_row.text: Forwarded-header parsing, root_path composition, and scope stripping now use one shared normalization contract across HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and WebSocket carriers.

claims.title: TC-CONTRACT-PROXY-PRECEDENCE
claims.description: Forwarded and X-Forwarded-* precedence is explicit for client, scheme, host, and root_path resolution across the package-owned HTTP and WebSocket scope builders.

source_row.text: Forwarded and X-Forwarded-* precedence is explicit for client, scheme, host, and root_path resolution across the package-owned HTTP and WebSocket scope builders.

claims.title: TC-CONTRACT-PROXY-TRUST
claims.description: Trusted proxy admission is explicit and fail-closed; proxy identity headers are ignored unless proxy handling is enabled and the immediate peer is trusted.

source_row.text: Trusted proxy admission is explicit and fail-closed; proxy identity headers are ignored unless proxy handling is enabled and the immediate peer is trusted.

claims.title: TC-DIFF-TLS13-STDLIB-CONTROL
claims.description: The stdlib TLS backend exists as a bounded differential control and emergency fallback, without displacing the package-owned TLS claim.

source_row.text: The stdlib TLS backend exists as a bounded differential control and emergency fallback, without displacing the package-owned TLS claim.

claims.title: TC-FIELD-DEFAULT-PRESENCE-PACKAGE-OWNED
claims.description: Package-owned HTTP, TLS, WebSocket, and adjacent trace field families have a documented and tested default presence, suppression, and pass-through posture.

source_row.text: Package-owned HTTP, TLS, WebSocket, and adjacent trace field families have a documented and tested default presence, suppression, and pass-through posture.

claims.title: TC-FIELD-DEFAULT-TERMINATION-PACKAGE-OWNED
claims.description: Package-owned HTTP, TLS, WebSocket, and adjacent trace field families have a frozen default termination, forwarding, stripping, and regeneration contract across supported carriers.

source_row.text: Package-owned HTTP, TLS, WebSocket, and adjacent trace field families have a frozen default termination, forwarding, stripping, and regeneration contract across supported carriers.

claims.title: TC-FIELD-OBSOLETED-ABSENCE-DEFAULT
claims.description: Obsoleted HTTP fields that are outside the current surface are absent by default and are not synthesized by normalization paths.

source_row.text: Obsoleted HTTP fields that are outside the current surface are absent by default and are not synthesized by normalization paths.

claims.title: TC-GOV-DEFAULT-AUDIT-POLICY
claims.description: Generated default audits are governed by explicit package-owned authority that keeps code, generated audits, and operator tables aligned.

source_row.text: Generated default audits are governed by explicit package-owned authority that keeps code, generated audits, and operator tables aligned.

claims.title: TC-GOV-RISK-REGISTER-TRACEABILITY
claims.description: Risk ownership and traceability are formalized as machine-readable release inputs with explicit schema, policy, and referential-integrity checks.

source_row.text: Risk ownership and traceability are formalized as machine-readable release inputs with explicit schema, policy, and referential-integrity checks.

claims.title: TC-GOV-TEST-STYLE-POLICY
claims.description: Pytest is the sole forward runner while legacy unittest coverage is frozen behind an explicit approved inventory.

source_row.text: Pytest is the sole forward runner while legacy unittest coverage is frozen behind an explicit approved inventory.

claims.title: TC-INTEROP-TLS13-CURL-OPENSSL35
claims.description: Tigrcorn custom TLS 1.3 interoperates with curl built against OpenSSL 3.5+ for HTTPS requests in the declared boundary. This umbrella row is tracked through atomic RFC claim rows rather than treated as a single indivisible correctness claim.

source_row.text: Tigrcorn custom TLS 1.3 interoperates with curl built against OpenSSL 3.5+ for HTTPS requests in the declared boundary. This umbrella row is tracked through atomic RFC claim rows rather than treated as a single indivisible correctness claim.

claims.title: TC-INTEROP-TLS13-OPENSSL35-SCLIENT
claims.description: Tigrcorn custom TLS 1.3 interoperates with OpenSSL s_client 3.5+ for the declared TCP/TLS boundary. This umbrella row is tracked through atomic RFC claim rows rather than treated as a single indivisible correctness claim.

source_row.text: Tigrcorn custom TLS 1.3 interoperates with OpenSSL s_client 3.5+ for the declared TCP/TLS boundary. This umbrella row is tracked through atomic RFC claim rows rather than treated as a single indivisible correctness claim.

claims.title: TC-NEG-ADVERSARIAL-CORPORA
claims.description: Proxy, early-data, QUIC, origin, CONNECT relay, TLS/X.509, and mixed-topology adversarial suites are preserved with explicit expected outcomes so negative behavior is replayable rather than anecdotal.

source_row.text: Proxy, early-data, QUIC, origin, CONNECT relay, TLS/X.509, and mixed-topology adversarial suites are preserved with explicit expected outcomes so negative behavior is replayable rather than anecdotal.

claims.title: TC-NEG-BUNDLE-PRESERVATION
claims.description: Expected-outcome bundles for all risky surfaces are serialized as machine-readable artifacts and linked to preserved historical release-root negative bundles where those bundles already exist.

source_row.text: Expected-outcome bundles for all risky surfaces are serialized as machine-readable artifacts and linked to preserved historical release-root negative bundles where those bundles already exist.

claims.title: TC-NEG-FAIL-STATE-REGISTRY
claims.description: Reject, close, abort, strip-and-continue, and gate-reject behavior for risky surfaces is frozen in a fail-state registry rather than inferred from scattered tests.

source_row.text: Reject, close, abort, strip-and-continue, and gate-reject behavior for risky surfaces is frozen in a fail-state registry rather than inferred from scattered tests.

claims.title: TC-OBS-EXPORT-ADAPTERS
claims.description: StatsD, DogStatsD, and OpenTelemetry export adapters are explicit supported operator controls with declared schema versions, accepted configuration forms, and bounded failure behavior.

source_row.text: StatsD, DogStatsD, and OpenTelemetry export adapters are explicit supported operator controls with declared schema versions, accepted configuration forms, and bounded failure behavior.

claims.title: TC-OBS-METRICS-SCHEMA
claims.description: QUIC and HTTP/3 counter families have stable package-owned names and semantics for transport, security, retry, migration, loss, and HTTP/3 state behavior.

source_row.text: QUIC and HTTP/3 counter families have stable package-owned names and semantics for transport, security, retry, migration, loss, and HTTP/3 state behavior.

claims.title: TC-OBS-QLOG-EXPERIMENTAL
claims.description: qlog export remains explicit, unstable, versioned, and redacted rather than being treated as a stable compatibility surface.

source_row.text: qlog export remains explicit, unstable, versioned, and redacted rather than being treated as a stable compatibility surface.

claims.title: TC-OPERATOR-CWD-IMPORT-RESOLUTION
claims.description: Package-owned app loading resolves module and factory targets from the current working directory when app.app_dir is unset.

claims.description: ALPN offer ordering and negotiation posture is a reviewed public TLS policy surface with synchronized CLI, defaults, and generated docs.

claims.title: TC-POLICY-CONNECT
claims.description: CONNECT relay admission is a reviewed public contract with explicit deny, relay, and allowlist posture across the package-owned protocol carriers.

source_row.text: CONNECT relay admission is a reviewed public contract with explicit deny, relay, and allowlist posture across the package-owned protocol carriers.

claims.title: TC-POLICY-CONTENT-CODING
claims.description: Content-coding policy and coding allowlists are explicit public controls rather than hidden normalization behavior.

source_row.text: Content-coding policy and coding allowlists are explicit public controls rather than hidden normalization behavior.

claims.title: TC-POLICY-DRAIN-ADMISSION
claims.description: Concurrency limits, stream admission, and graceful drain timing are explicit public operator controls rather than implicit scheduler behavior.

source_row.text: Concurrency limits, stream admission, and graceful drain timing are explicit public operator controls rather than implicit scheduler behavior.

claims.title: TC-POLICY-H2C
claims.description: H2C upgrade and direct-preface behavior is exposed as an explicit reviewed policy surface instead of an implicit transport default.

source_row.text: H2C upgrade and direct-preface behavior is exposed as an explicit reviewed policy surface instead of an implicit transport default.

claims.title: TC-POLICY-LIMITS-TIMEOUTS
claims.description: Runtime resource, buffering, and timeout controls are promoted as reviewed public policy surface rather than hidden scheduler or transport internals.

source_row.text: Runtime resource, buffering, and timeout controls are promoted as reviewed public policy surface rather than hidden scheduler or transport internals.

claims.title: TC-POLICY-REVOCATION
claims.description: OCSP, CRL, and online revocation-fetch controls are explicit public policy flags with generated operator documentation.

claims.title: TC-POLICY-TRAILERS
claims.description: Trailer handling posture is explicit and documented as a stable policy surface across HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, and HTTP/3.

source_row.text: Trailer handling posture is explicit and documented as a stable policy surface across HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, and HTTP/3.

claims.title: TC-POLICY-WEBSOCKET-COMPRESSION
claims.description: WebSocket compression posture is a stable public policy surface shared across the supported HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, and HTTP/3 WebSocket carriers.

source_row.text: WebSocket compression posture is a stable public policy surface shared across the supported HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, and HTTP/3 WebSocket carriers.

claims.title: TC-POLICY-WEBSOCKET-HEARTBEAT
claims.description: WebSocket heartbeat interval and timeout are explicit public controls with carrier-parity documentation and tests.

source_row.text: WebSocket heartbeat interval and timeout are explicit public controls with carrier-parity documentation and tests.

claims.title: TC-PROFILE-DEFAULT-BASELINE
claims.description: A default safe baseline profile freezes boring zero-config posture for TCP plus HTTP/1.1, no proxy trust unless configured, no H2/H3/QUIC unless explicit, CONNECT denied, server header off, and early data denied or not applicable.

source_row.text: A default safe baseline profile freezes boring zero-config posture for TCP plus HTTP/1.1, no proxy trust unless configured, no H2/H3/QUIC unless explicit, CONNECT denied, server header off, and early data denied or not applicable.

claims.title: TC-PROFILE-STATIC-ORIGIN
claims.description: A static-origin profile freezes static roots, index rules, validators, range behavior, compression interaction, and traversal or symlink posture.

source_row.text: A static-origin profile freezes static roots, index rules, validators, range behavior, compression interaction, and traversal or symlink posture.

claims.title: TC-PROFILE-STRICT-H1-ORIGIN
claims.description: A strict HTTP/1.1 origin profile freezes conservative origin semantics, trusted-proxy normalization, and explicit static/pathsend posture.

source_row.text: A strict HTTP/1.1 origin profile freezes conservative origin semantics, trusted-proxy normalization, and explicit static/pathsend posture.

claims.title: TC-PROFILE-STRICT-H2-ORIGIN
claims.description: A strict HTTP/2 origin profile freezes TLS plus ALPN plus H2 defaults and explicit frame, header, stream, and window bounds.

source_row.text: A strict HTTP/2 origin profile freezes TLS plus ALPN plus H2 defaults and explicit frame, header, stream, and window bounds.

claims.title: TC-PROFILE-STRICT-H3-EDGE
claims.description: A strict HTTP/3 edge profile freezes QUIC listener posture, Retry, migration, resumption, Alt-Svc posture, and default 0-RTT denial.

claims.title: TC-PROFILE-STRICT-MTLS-ORIGIN
claims.description: A strict mTLS origin profile freezes client-certificate requirement, SAN/EKU policy, revocation mode, and hard-fail versus soft-fail behavior.

claims.title: TC-RFC5280-AKI-SKI-CERT-CHAIN-MATERIAL
claims.description: Demo and certification certificate chains include Authority Key Identifier and Subject Key Identifier material needed for modern verifier acceptance.

source_row.text: Demo and certification certificate chains include Authority Key Identifier and Subject Key Identifier material needed for modern verifier acceptance.

claims.title: TC-RFC5280-KEYUSAGE-EKU-CORRECTNESS
claims.description: Leaf and intermediate certificates carry KeyUsage and ExtendedKeyUsage values appropriate for server authentication and mTLS where Tigrcorn claims those profiles.

source_row.text: Leaf and intermediate certificates carry KeyUsage and ExtendedKeyUsage values appropriate for server authentication and mTLS where Tigrcorn claims those profiles.

claims.title: TC-RFC5280-PATH-VALIDATION-CORRECTNESS
claims.description: Chain building, validity checks, basic constraints, and issuer linkage pass strict external peer validation for the certificate profiles Tigrcorn uses.

claims.title: TC-RFC6066-SNI-HANDLING
claims.description: Server Name Indication handling is correct for hostname-based TLS identity and certificate selection.

source_row.text: Server Name Indication handling is correct for hostname-based TLS identity and certificate selection.

claims.title: TC-RFC6066-STATUS-REQUEST-POLICY
claims.description: TLS `status_request` behavior is explicitly supported, denied, or policy-gated rather than ambiguous when strict peers request OCSP stapling behavior.

source_row.text: TLS `status_request` behavior is explicitly supported, denied, or policy-gated rather than ambiguous when strict peers request OCSP stapling behavior.

claims.title: TC-RFC6455-WS-ACCEPT-EXTENSION-NEGOTIATION-DISCIPLINE
claims.description: WebSocket accept handling rejects direct application override of extension-negotiation headers and keeps extension negotiation package-owned.

source_row.text: WebSocket accept handling rejects direct application override of extension-negotiation headers and keeps extension negotiation package-owned.

claims.title: TC-RFC6960-OCSP-POLICY-EXPLICITNESS
claims.description: OCSP hard-fail, soft-fail, and responder-unavailable policy is explicit and testable wherever Tigrcorn claims OCSP behavior.

source_row.text: OCSP hard-fail, soft-fail, and responder-unavailable policy is explicit and testable wherever Tigrcorn claims OCSP behavior.

claims.title: TC-RFC7301-ALPN-NEGOTIATION-POLICY
claims.description: ALPN offer, selection, and denial behavior is explicit and externally testable for the H1 and H2 TLS lanes Tigrcorn claims.

claims.title: TC-RFC7301-ALPN-NORMALIZATION-EMPTY-INPUT
claims.description: Empty ALPN inputs normalize to no negotiated protocol rather than an empty-string protocol identifier.

source_row.text: Empty ALPN inputs normalize to no negotiated protocol rather than an empty-string protocol identifier.

claims.title: TC-RFC8446-CERTIFICATE-AND-CERTIFICATEVERIFY-PROCESSING
claims.description: TLS 1.3 `Certificate` and `CertificateVerify` processing, including signature-algorithm negotiation and verification behavior, interoperates with strict external peers.

source_row.text: TLS 1.3 `Certificate` and `CertificateVerify` processing, including signature-algorithm negotiation and verification behavior, interoperates with strict external peers.

claims.title: TC-RFC8446-TLS13-AEAD-ADDITIONAL-DATA
claims.description: TLS 1.3 AEAD additional authenticated data exactly matches the protected record-header semantics expected by strict peers.

source_row.text: TLS 1.3 AEAD additional authenticated data exactly matches the protected record-header semantics expected by strict peers.

claims.title: TC-RFC8446-TLS13-ALERT-AND-CLOSE-SEMANTICS
claims.description: TLS 1.3 fatal alerts, `close_notify` behavior, and post-failure shutdown semantics are externally intelligible and spec-correct.

source_row.text: TLS 1.3 fatal alerts, `close_notify` behavior, and post-failure shutdown semantics are externally intelligible and spec-correct.

claims.title: TC-RFC8446-TLS13-HANDSHAKE-TO-APPDATA-BOUNDARY
claims.description: TLS 1.3 transitions from handshake traffic keys to application-data traffic keys at the correct boundary after `Finished`, and the first protected application record is emitted under the correct keys.

source_row.text: TLS 1.3 transitions from handshake traffic keys to application-data traffic keys at the correct boundary after `Finished`, and the first protected application record is emitted under the correct keys.

claims.title: TC-RFC8446-TLS13-INNER-CONTENT-TYPE-RECOVERY
claims.description: TLS 1.3 `TLSInnerPlaintext` encoding preserves the correct inner content type and permits correct post-decrypt recovery by strict peers.

source_row.text: TLS 1.3 `TLSInnerPlaintext` encoding preserves the correct inner content type and permits correct post-decrypt recovery by strict peers.

claims.title: TC-RFC8446-TLS13-PADDING-SEMANTICS
claims.description: TLS 1.3 protected-record zero-padding handling is correct, unambiguous, and interoperable with strict external peers.

source_row.text: TLS 1.3 protected-record zero-padding handling is correct, unambiguous, and interoperable with strict external peers.

claims.title: TC-RFC8446-TLS13-PROTECTED-RECORD-OUTER-FRAMING
claims.description: TLS 1.3 protected records emit correct `TLSCiphertext` outer framing, including outer content type, legacy record version handling, and record length accounting expected by strict peers.

claims.description: QUIC Retry and token validation remain correct when TLS transcript or key-schedule behavior changes.

claims.title: TC-RFC9001-QUIC-TLS-MAPPING-PARITY
claims.description: The custom TLS implementation preserves the required TLS 1.3 semantics when mapped into Tigrcorn's QUIC implementation.

source_row.text: The custom TLS implementation preserves the required TLS 1.3 semantics when mapped into Tigrcorn's QUIC implementation.

claims.title: TC-RFC9002-QUIC-DEFERRED-SEND-PATH

claims.title: TC-RFC9112-HTTPS-HTTP11-INTEROPERABILITY

claims.title: TC-RFC9113-HTTP2-DEFAULT-INITIALIZATION

claims.title: TC-RFC9113-HTTP2-OVER-TLS-POSTURE

claims.title: TC-RFC9114-H3-CONTROL-PLANE-AFTER-TLS-SUCCESS
claims.description: HTTP/3 promotion-facing control-plane claims are made only after TLS-backed QUIC handshakes are externally reproducible against independent peers.

claims.title: TC-RFC9204-QPACK-AFTER-STABLE-H3-HANDSHAKE
claims.description: QPACK pressure and decode-failure behavior is promoted only after external-peer HTTP/3 handshake stability exists.

claims.description: Certificates used for public HTTPS interop satisfy modern DNS-ID and SAN-based hostname verification expectations.

claims.title: TC-ROADMAP-P8-PYTEST-FORWARD

risks.title: Interop and performance bundles could drift out of the release gate if they are treated as informal side artifacts.
risks.description: The mutable tree now carries explicit retained-bundle manifests that point at canonical release-root evidence and performance inputs.

risks.title: Structured-fields references can drift back to obsolete predecessor wording.
risks.description: Phase 8 replaces active predecessor-baseline language with RFC 9651 and lints for stale active references.

risks.title: New forward tests could continue to enter as unittest instead of pytest.
risks.description: Pytest is the only forward runner in CI, while legacy unittest files are frozen behind an approved inventory.

risks.title: Risk, claim, test, and evidence linkage can drift without machine-readable ownership.
risks.description: Phase 8 closes this by generating risk and traceability graphs and making release gates validate them.

boundaries.title: Authoritative Tigrcorn frozen boundary and governance graph

adrs.title: Canonical registry is a single JSON document
Entity-centric registry with a derived graph export
Claim status and claim tier are orthogonal
Feature implementation state is separate from lifecycle state
Portable core with repo-specific evidence generation outside the core
ADR-1003: scheduler and backpressure are separate concerns
ADR-1004: custom scope types are allowed behind the ASGI surface

specs.title: Public operator and programmatic surface reference
